events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess rigging configuration: hook latch missing/tied back. Staged. Need choose correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed wrong. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Hook latch missing/tied back is critical for rigging safety; if latch missing/tied back, sling could disengage. I'd set critical. Work_stage staged. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 0 since staged not begun. Value 0. Confidence 1.0. Findings: hook latch missing/tied back; latch is required to retain sling/load attachment; tied back defeats self-closing function. Actions: remove hook from service, replace with properly latched self-closing safety hook or repair/replace latch, inspect all hooks, ensure latches are functional and not tied back, verify per ASME B30.10/OSHA 1926.251. Rationale: ASME B30.10 requires hooks equipped with self-closing latch or moused; OSHA 1926.251 requires safe rigging, hooks with safety latches. Missing/tied-back latch creates risk of load detachment. References: ASME B30.10, OSHA 1926.251. Need include order, workable, sound_source, event_time, rate. For non-audio tasks, use defaults? Schema says use for audio/video-native tasks when requested. We can set empty arrays/strings/0. order: []? workable: []? sound_source: "", event_time:0.0, rate:0.0. Ensure valid JSON.

  "findings": [
    "Hook safety latch is missing or tied back, defeating the self-closing retention feature.",
    "A hook without a functional latch cannot reliably retain slings or attachments during lifting.",
    "This condition violates rigging safety requirements before lifting begins."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the defective hook from service immediately.",
    "Replace the hook with a properly rated hook equipped with a functional self-closing safety latch, or repair/replace the missing latch per manufacturer requirements.",
    "Inspect all rigging hooks on site for missing, damaged, or tied-back latches.",
    "Do not begin lifting until all hooks are verified compliant and latches operate freely."
  ],
  "rationale": "ASME B30.10 and OSHA 1926.251 require hooks used in rigging to have a safety latch or equivalent means to prevent unintentional disengagement of the load attachment. A missing or tied-back latch creates a direct risk of the sling or load detaching during a lift, so the configuration is non-compliant and unsafe.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.10",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
